list_servers.description: Search and browse public MCP servers with full-text/semantic search, pagination, and filtering
list_servers.test_result: Success - retrieved 3 servers of 6,358 total from public API

create_server.description: Create a new server entry. Idempotent.

publish_server.description: Submit a release via multipart form. Supports hosted (JS module), external (URL), and stdio (MCPB bundle) release types.

update_server.description: Update server metadata such as display name, description, repository, icon, or visibility.

get_server.description: Retrieve server details including connections, tools, and security status.

interactive_form_details.description: Web-based form for publishing servers via URL or local bundle. Requires browser interaction.
interactive_form_details.cli_alternative: smithery mcp publish command available as alternative

heavy_javascript_details.main_site: The main smithery.ai website uses Next.js with significant JavaScript for rendering
heavy_javascript_details.api: The API at api.smithery.ai returns pure JSON and requires no JavaScript processing
heavy_javascript_details.docs: Documentation at smithery.ai/docs is built with Mintlify (Next.js) but content is also accessible via LLMs.txt format

publish_methods.description: REST API at api.smithery.ai for programmatic server creation and publishing
publish_methods.notes: Requires API key authentication for write operations. Best for automated workflows.

publish_methods.description: Smithery CLI tool for publishing from command line
publish_methods.notes: Use `smithery mcp publish` command. Handles authentication via API key.
Interactive form for manual submission. Not suitable for automation.

recommended_method_details.rationale: Smithery provides a well-documented, comprehensive REST JSON API at api.smithery.ai. No CAPTCHA, no JavaScript rendering required, no interactive form fills. The API supports all operations needed: listing servers (public, no auth), creating servers (with API key), and publishing releases (with API key). This is the most efficient and reliable method for automation.
steps: 1. Obtain a Smithery API key from the Smithery dashboard
2. Create a server: PUT https://api.smithery.ai/servers/{qualifiedName} with JSON body (displayName, description)
3. Publish a release: PUT https://api.smithery.ai/servers/{qualifiedName}/releases with multipart form data including JSON payload
4. For URL-based hosting (simplest): Set payload type to 'external' with the server URL
5. For stdio servers: Upload an MCPB bundle file

conclusion: Smithery is the most API-friendly MCP directory. It has a fully documented REST API, no CAPTCHAs, no JavaScript rendering requirements for API operations, and supports programmatic listing, creation, and publishing of MCP servers. The API method is strongly recommended for automation.
